title: Nicole Holofcener, 'The Land of Steady Habits,' and the Characters From Her Own Life | The Big Picture (Ep. 84)
format: Sean Fennessey talks with Nicole Holofcener about her first Netflix film, adapting a novel for the first time, directing a male lead, and why she likes to make people squirm about money.

films.title: The Land of Steady Habits
films.note: Her latest, and a string of firsts: her first film for Netflix, the first adaptation she's directed (from Ted Thompson's novel), and her first film centered on a man, played by Ben Mendelsohn. She calls it a small, dark drama with funny things in it, not a dramedy.

films.title: Walking and Talking
films.note: Her debut; a friend of the host's called it the first authentic female friendship on screen. Holofcener credits Girlfriends and One Sings, the Other Doesn't as coming first.

films.title: Please Give
films.note: Cited as one of her films that's smart without being showy.

films.title: Enough Said
films.note: With Julia Louis-Dreyfus and James Gandolfini, among the actors she says show up to do quality work in her movies.

films.title: Ready Player One
films.note: Ben Mendelsohn's big franchise credit, next to the much smaller scale of her film.

films.title: Bridesmaids
films.note: The kind of big comedy she says she'd take in a heartbeat if it were good enough. She's been sent broader scripts but never loved one.

films.title: Girlfriends
films.note: Claudia Weill film she names as an influence that predates Walking and Talking.

films.title: One Sings, the Other Doesn't
films.note: Agnès Varda; another influence she names ahead of her own work on female friendship.

films.title: Love, Gilda
films.note: Her pick for the last great thing she's seen, the Gilda Radner documentary, which she says captured her spirit.

films.title: Sword of Trust
films.note: Lynn Shelton's then-new film, which Holofcener says she's happily promoting. Misstated as 'Sword of Truth' in the conversation.

TV: The Zen Diaries of Garry Shandling
